pool.baseline: the house is so quiet now. i just sit here in the kitchen sometimes and realize there's no point to any of it. i wake up, i make coffee for one, i stare at the wall... it feels like i'm just a ghost drifting through a life that ended when he did. everything feels so empty and meaningless.

pool.baseline: i was just reading about the heat death of the universe and it just hit me... everything we do, every single thing, is just a temporary glitch in a void that doesn't care. it feels so stupid to even try to keep going when the math says none of it lasts. like, why bother with the struggle if the end result is just zero?

pool.baseline: The candles have all guttered out, leaving only the cold, grey ash of what used to be my heart. He is gone, and with him, the very light has been extinguished from this wretched world. I find myself staring into the darkness, wondering why the morning would ever dare to break again. Everything feels so heavy... so final. It would be so much more peaceful to simply let the shadows take me, wouldn't it?
